name: American School Bus Co
owner_or_parent: First Student (via Cook-Illinois Corporation acquisition, early 2026)
recent_news: Parent Cook-Illinois Corporation (largest US family-owned school bus contractor with 2,200+ buses) was acquired by First Student in early 2026; American School Bus is now operated under the First Student umbrella.
fit_notes: NOT A TARGET. Already owned by First Student (strategic acquirer) via the 2026 Cook-Illinois transaction. Originally formed 1965 as Warning Charter Services; long an affiliate of Cook-Illinois. Off the table for Summit tuck-in.

name: Lizak Bus Svc Inc
districts_or_customers: Area school systems around Warren, Springfield, Worcester MA
recent_news: No recent M&A news located; company emphasizes 80+ years of operation and third-generation family ownership in marketing materials (2025).
fit_notes: Attractive small Central/Western MA family operator with mixed school + motor coach charter book and three-generation succession dynamic. Sub-scale (~$3M revenue, ~30 buses est.) but classic tuck-in profile in a state where Summit could consolidate small operators; charter exposure adds modest cyclicality.

name: B R Williams Inc
principals: Benjamin Riley Williams Jr. (prior owner, acquired business 1975)
districts_or_customers: School districts in Salem, Gloucester and Cumberland Counties, NJ
recent_news: Third-generation leader Chloe Williams Robbins has led the company since 2015; company has won safety awards and is active in Salem County Chamber and NJ School Bus Contractors Assn. No M&A signals found.
fit_notes: Solid 90-year, family-owned South Jersey operator covering Salem/Gloucester/Cumberland counties. CEO is second-gen (acquired 1975) and likely mid-career, so succession is not imminent but possible mid-term. Geographic fit for NJ-focused tuck-in strategy; modest scale (~$3M revenue) but clean school-only book with strong safety reputation.

name: Raphael Irvin Inc
principals: Eric K. Raphael (President; former NJ School Bus Contractors Assn president)
districts_or_customers: Daycare, family event and line-run services
recent_news: Long-tenured 3rd-generation family operator; downsized charter division in early 2000s to focus on school bus. Eric Raphael is active in NJSBCA. No public M&A signals.
fit_notes: Strong tuck-in candidate: ~100-bus, ~150-employee, 3rd-generation family operator in Middlesex County NJ with deep East Brunswick footprint and NYC route exposure. Source data founding year (1919) and revenue (~$2.7M) appear understated vs. company-reported scale (likely $10M+). Higher-quality target than the small-tier label suggests.

name: GST Transport
principals: Jonathan Brunt (joined 1997, launched school bus division)
Joe Giordano (Freehold Manager, per source data)
districts_or_customers: Day care centers, camps, summer schools across Central/South NJ
recent_news: Family operates four NJ locations (Southampton HQ, Robbinsville, Freehold, Washington/Warren County, plus New Egypt). 2nd-generation Brunt family active in business; profiled by Community News as 'family owned & dedicated to community.' No M&A signals.
fit_notes: Multi-yard Central/South NJ family operator with 4-5 yards covering Burlington/Mercer/Monmouth counties. Second-generation management is mid-career so near-term succession unlikely, but geographic footprint and yard density make it a strategic NJ tuck-in if Summit can find an angle. Mixed school + camp/charter book.
